trails.name: Frew Hut to Whitcombe Road End Track
Ross Goldfield Water Race Walkway
trails.description: Discover old gold mining relics or enjoy 360 degrees on this walk with interesting options suitable for children.

trails.name: Mahinapua short walks
trails.description: These short walks pass through dense bush to reach Lake Mahinapua - with a picnic area/campsite at one end, these walks are great for kids.

trails.name: Mananui Tramline (Mahinapua Walkway)
trails.description: This flat, easy walk or mountain bike ride, suitable for children, follows an old logging tramline and passes beautiful Lake Mahinapua.

trails.name: Water Race Walkway
trails.description:
The Ross Water Race Walkway is part of the Ross Historic Goldfields Walkway and journeys through regenerating native bush, past relics from the 1865 gold rush and through the original Ross cemetery. The walkway follows the remains of a network of water-races, which supplied water to the sluicing operations of the goldfields during the 1865 gold rush.

The Ross Goldfields Information and Heritage Centre, located at the northern end of the walkway, provides interesting background information on the history of the Ross Goldfields.

trails.name: Hokitika Heritage Walk
trails.description: Goldrush and river-port town historic heritage sites, buildings and statues. Brochure and information panels bring history to life.

trails.name: Prossers Bush
trails.description: A few different side paths through trees.

trails.name: Racecourse Walkway Track
trails.description: Connects with other walks such as Industrial Road and provides commuter access to Brickfield Road from town.

trails.name: Rimu Woodland Lookout Area + Lightning Track
trails.description: From Hokitika drive down either Kaniere Road, turn right to cross the Kaniere Bridge and connect to Woodstock Rimu Road. Alternatively drive south over Hokitika Bridge on SH6 and take a left turn onto Arthurstown Road then turn right onto Woodstock Rimu Road. Once on Woodstock Rimu Road, keep driving until you go up a hill and pass the Woodstock Royal Mail pub on your right. Turn left at top of that hill and enter marked carpark area called Rimu Lookout.
